title: dortmanna anceps
intro: Dortmanna anceps, also known as the two-toothed dodder, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Apocynaceae. This plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a diuretic and anti-inflammatory agent. The health benefits of Dortmanna anceps include its potential to treat urinary issues, such as kidney stones and bladder infections, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of Dortmanna anceps can be made by infusing the plant's leaves and stems in hot water or by using it as a tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
